title: The BoJ hiked a policy rate and will end the tapering of JGB purchases in FY2027
gist: The Bank of Japan raised its policy rate from 0.75% to 1% on June 16, 2026 and signaled it will end the tapering of JGB purchases in FY2027; Oxford Economics expects gradual ~semiannual hikes toward a 1.5% terminal/neutral rate while the BoJ faces a yen-versus-domestic-demand dilemma.

themes.theme: Policy rate hike and normalization path

insights.claim: The Bank of Japan raised its policy rate from 0.75% to 1% on June 16, 2026, despite uncertainty around the Middle East conflict, and kept forward guidance that it will continue interest rate normalization.
insights.speaker: Yasuko Koido
insights.so_what: Confirms the BoJ is pressing ahead with tightening through geopolitical risk, a hawkish signal for JGB yields, the yen, and global carry trades.
insights.quote: The Bank of Japan (BoJ) raised its policy rate from 0.75% to 1% on June 16 despite the uncertainty around the Middle East conflict.

insights.claim: Oxford Economics projects the Bank of Japan will hike gradually, roughly every six months, until reaching a terminal rate of 1.5%, which is its estimate of Japan's neutral rate.
insights.speaker: Yasuko Koido
insights.so_what: Sets a concrete rate trajectory for positioning in JGBs and JPY; a 1.5% terminal rate caps how far Japanese yields can rise on policy alone.
insights.quote: the pace of subsequent hikes will be gradual, occurring every six months or so, until the terminal rate of 1.5%, our estimate of the neutral rate, is reached

themes.theme: FX pressure and the BoJ dilemma

insights.claim: Persistent FX-market perception that the Bank of Japan is behind the curve will likely keep pressure on the central bank to hike faster and higher in coming quarters.
insights.speaker: Yasuko Koido
insights.so_what: Market pressure could force a steeper-than-guided hiking path, a risk for JGB shorts and yen carry trades to monitor.
insights.quote: the persistent perception in FX markets that the BoJ is behind the curve will likely continue to put pressure on the central bank to hike the policy rate faster and higher
insights.locator: However, the persistent perception in FX

insights.claim: As Japan's policy rate approaches the neutral rate, the Bank of Japan will face a dilemma over how much negative impact on domestic demand and political pressure it can tolerate in order to curb yen depreciation.
insights.speaker: Yasuko Koido
insights.so_what: Frames the trade-off that limits BoJ tightening, signaling the yen could stay weak if domestic demand constrains further hikes.
insights.quote: a difficult dilemma regarding how much of a negative impact on domestic demand and the associated political pressures it can tolerate in order to curb yen depreciation

insights.claim: The Bank of Japan decided to end the tapering of its Japanese government bond purchases in fiscal year 2027 to improve the functioning and stability of JGB markets, without referencing recent high volatility in global yields, and will amend the pace of purchases only when deemed necessary.
insights.speaker: Yasuko Koido
insights.so_what: An end to taper in FY2027 means the BoJ's balance-sheet runoff has a defined horizon, relevant for JGB supply-demand and long-end yields.
insights.quote: with a view to improve the functioning and stability of the JGB markets
insights.locator: The BoJ decided to end the tapering
